content.cn_text: 菲比，你的校本研究进行得怎么样了?
content.en_text: So how did you get on with your school-based research, Phoebe?

content.cn_text: 虽然很累，但很有价值。
content.en_text: Well, it was exhausting but really valuable.

content.cn_text: 好。你选择的具体重点是什么?
content.en_text: Good. What was the specific focus you chose?

content.cn_text: 我的题目是“11 - 12岁学生的学习态度”。
content.en_text: My title is 'Attitudes towards study among eleven-twelve year-old pupils'.

content.cn_text: 正确的。是什么让你选择了这个重点?
content.en_text: Right. And what made you choose that focus?

content.cn_text: 嗯，这有点难……我的很多同学很早就决定了他们的重点……主要是基于他们认为这对他们未来的职业生涯有帮助，你知道，在他们第一年的教学中。
content.en_text: Well, that's a bit difficult ... lots of my classmates decided on their focus really early on...mainly on the basis of what they thought would help in their future career, you know,in their first year's teaching.

content.cn_text: 这就是帮助你做出决定的原因?
content.en_text: So that's what helped you decide?

content.cn_text: 实际上，是我偶然看到一本由经验丰富的老师写的关于学生态度的书，这促使我去研究这个话题。
content.en_text: Actually, it was that I came across a book written by experienced teachers on student attitudes and that motivated me to go for the topic.

content.cn_text: 那么你的研究问题是什么?
content.en_text: So what were your research questions or issues?

content.cn_text: 我想看看学生对不同老师的反应，特别关注严厉的老师是否会让青少年缺乏动力。
content.en_text: Well I wanted to look at the ways students responded to different teachers particularly focusing on whether very strict teachers made teenagers less motivated.

content.cn_text: 根据你的研究，你发现这是真的吗?
content.en_text: And, from your research, did you find that was true?

content.cn_text: 不，从我所看到的你知道，从我五天的观察，与人交谈等等。
content.en_text: No, not from what I saw you know, from my five days' observation, talking to people and so forth.

content.cn_text: 好吧……我们稍后会讨论实际的研究方法，但在此之前，你能简要总结一下你最引人注目的发现是什么吗?
content.en_text: OK ... We'll talk about the actual research methods in a moment, but before that, can you briefly summarise what your most striking findings are.

content.cn_text: 嗯，真正让我惊讶的是显著的性别差异，我并没有开始关注这一点，但我发现男孩对上学的态度要积极得多……女孩们更没有耐心，她们说了很多想要长大离开学校的话。
content.en_text: Well, what really amazed me was the significant gender differences, I didn't set out to focus on that but I found that boys were much more positive about being at school... girls were more impatient, they talked a lot about wanting to grow up and leave school.

content.cn_text: 很有趣。
content.en_text: Very interesting.

content.cn_text: 是的…它是。从做研究的过程中，我很清楚，你可能一开始专注于一件事，但你会得到很多意想不到的见解。
content.en_text: Yeah ... it is. From doing the research it was clear to me that you might start out to focus on one thing but you pick up lots of unexpected insights.

content.cn_text: 正确的。你对教学有什么见解吗?
content.en_text: Right. Did you get any insights into teaching?

content.cn_text: 是的,当然可以。我做了很多关于不同能力的孩子在特定任务上的合作方式的观察，你知道，互相帮助，我开始意识到课程正在以意想不到的方式发展。
content.en_text: Yes, certainly. I was doing a lot of observations of the way kids with very different abilities collaborate on certain tasks, you know, help each other and I began to realise that the lessons were developing in really unexpected ways.

content.cn_text: 那么你从中得出了什么结论?
content.en_text: So what conclusion do you draw from that?

content.cn_text: 嗯，我知道老师认真备课是必要的，但如果他们也允许课程按照自己的方式进行，那就太好了。
content.en_text: Well, I know it's necessary for teachers to prepare lessons carefully but it's great if they also allow lessons to go their own ways.

content.cn_text: 好点。现在，我真的很高兴看到你在做这个——根据数据分析和得出结论。
content.en_text: Good point. Now, I'm really pleased to see you doing this - analysing and drawing conclusions based on data.

content.cn_text: 但这肯定不是正确的数据……
content.en_text: But surely this isn't proper data ...

content.cn_text: 因为它是从小规模的研究中得出的?好吧，只要你不夸大你的发现，这个数据是完全有效的。
content.en_text: Because it's derived from such small-scale research? Well, as long as you don't make grand claims for your findings, this data is entirely valid.

content.cn_text: 嗯。
content.en_text: Hmm .

content.cn_text: .我喜欢你从经历中退后一步，思考你从研究中学到了什么……做得很好。
content.en_text: I like the way you're already stepping back from the experience and thinking about what you've learned about research ... well done.

content.cn_text: 但我知道我可以做得更好。
content.en_text: But I know I could have done it better.

content.cn_text: 当你变得更有经验时，你会找到减少困难风险的方法。
content.en_text: As you become more experienced you'll find ways to reduce the risk of difficulties.

content.cn_text: 好的。
content.en_text: OK.

content.cn_text: 托尼:那么，让我们更详细地看看你是如何收集数据的。让我们从课堂观察开始。
content.en_text: Tony: So, let's look in more detail at how you gathered your data. Let's start with lesson observation.

content.cn_text: 菲比:嗯，总的来说很顺利。我选择了重点，设计了清单。然后老师允许我进入他们的课堂，没有任何问题，这让我很惊讶。直到后来，艰苦的工作才开始!
content.en_text: Phoebe: Well, it generally went quite smoothly. I chose my focus and designed my checklist. Then teachers allowed me into their classes without any problems, which surprised me. It was afterwards that the gruelling work started!

content.cn_text: 托尼:是啊，非常耗时，不是吗?理解……分析……你的观察笔记。
content.en_text: Tony: Yeah, it's very time consuming, isn't it? Making sense of...analysing ... your observation notes.

content.cn_text: 菲比:当然。例如，比面试数据更重要的是……这是相对容易处理的，尽管我想确保我使用的是高质量的录音机……为了让抄写更容易，我不得不等到有了一份。
content.en_text: Phoebe: Absolutely. Much more so than interview data, for example ... that was relatively easy to process, though I wanted to make sure I used a high-quality recorder ... to make transcription easier and I had to wait until one became available.

content.cn_text: 托尼:是的。你也采访了一些孩子吗?
content.en_text: Tony: Right. And did you interview some kids as well?

content.cn_text: 最后，是的，我和十个人谈过，他们都很好。我原以为我听他们会很无聊，但是…
content.en_text: Phoebe: In the end, yes, I talked to ten, and they were great. I'd imagined I'd be bored listening to them, but.. .

content.cn_text: 托尼:所以很容易集中注意力?
content.en_text: Tony: So it was easy to concentrate?

content.cn_text: 菲比:当然。其中一位老师有点担心道德问题，你知道，采访年轻学生是否正确，他花了一段时间才同意让我和他班上的三个孩子交谈，但最后他还是让步了。
content.en_text: Phoebe: Sure. One of the teachers was a bit worried about the ethics, you know,whether it was right to interview young pupils, and it took a while for him to agree to let me talk to three of the kids in his class but he relented in the end.

content.cn_text: 托尼:好的。你还用了什么其他方法?
content.en_text: Tony: Good. What other methods did you use?

content.cn_text: 我尝试过问卷调查，但我现在真的很后悔。我决定和另一个学生一起做，但我们的日程太不一样了，结果花了两倍的时间。
content.en_text: Phoebe: I experimented with questionnaires, but I really regret that now. I decided to share the work with another student but we had such different agendas it ended up taking twice as long.

content.cn_text: 托尼:真遗憾……在未来的项目中，你可能需要思考如何改进它。
content.en_text: Tony: That's a shame ... it might be worth you reflecting on ways you might improve on that for future projects .

content.cn_text: 你说得对。
content.en_text: Phoebe: You're right, yeah.

content.cn_text: 托尼:好的。
content.en_text: Tony: OK.

content.cn_text: 我做的另一件事是静物摄影。我没拿那么多
content.en_text: Phoebe: And the other thing I did was stills photography. I didn't take as many

content.cn_text: 像我希望的那样。
content.en_text: pictures as I'd hoped to .

content.cn_text: 托尼:没时间?
content.en_text: Tony: Lack of time?

content.cn_text: 菲比:这很简单，直接咬……但我希望每张照片都有一个目的，你知道，这将有助于我的研究目标，我发现这很难。
content.en_text: Phoebe: It's pretty easy just snapping away ... but I wanted each snap to have a purpose, you know, that would contribute to my research aims and I found that difficult.

content.cn_text: 托尼:嗯，这可以理解，但是记住……
content.en_text: Tony: Well, that's understandable, but remember...

groups.desc:
Questions 21-25
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

items.title: Phoebe's main reason for choosing her topic was that

options.title: A
options.content: her classmates had been very interested in it.

options.title: B
options.content: it would help prepare her for her first teaching post.

options.title: C
options.content: she had been inspired by a particular book.

items.analysis: Phoebe选择研究主题的主要原因是她偶然发现了一本书。这本书是由有经验的教师撰写的，内容关于学生态度。正是这本书激发了她的兴趣，促使她选择了这个研究主题。这直接对应了选项C，表明她是受到了一本特定书籍的启发。

items.title: Phoebe's main research question related to
items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A，因为原文中明确提到Phoebe的研究问题是关于严格的教师是否会使青少年的学习动力降低。这直接对应了选项A中提到的"教师纪律的影响"。题目要求找出主要研究问题，而原文中使用"particularly focusing on"（特别关注）这一表述，明确指出了这是研究的核心问题。

items.title: Phoebe was most surprised by her finding that

options.title: A
options.content: gender did not influence behaviour significantly.

options.title: B
options.content: girls were more negative about school than boys.

options.title: C
options.content: boys were more talkative than girls in class.

items.analysis: 文中明确指出Phoebe对发现的性别差异感到惊讶。她观察到男生对学校的态度更积极，而女生则表现得更加不耐烦，谈论着想要离开学校。这清楚地表明女生对学校的态度比男生更消极，因此B选项是正确答案。

items.title: Regarding teaching, Phoebe says she has learned that

options.title: A
options.content: teachers should be flexible in their lesson planning.

options.title: B
options.content: brighter children learn from supporting weaker ones.

options.title: C
options.content: children vary from each other in unpredictable ways.

items.analysis: Phoebe在谈到对教学的见解时，强调了教师应该在课堂上保持灵活性。她认识到仔细备课的重要性，但同时也指出允许课堂按照自己的方式发展是很好的。这直接对应了选项A中提到的教师在课堂计划中应该保持灵活性的观点。Phoebe的这个观点是基于她在课堂观察中看到的意外发展，这让她意识到灵活性的重要性。

items.title: Tony is particularly impressed by Phoebe's ability to

options.title: A
options.content: recognise the limitations of such small-scale research.

options.title: B
options.content: reflect on her own research experience in an interesting way.

options.title: C
options.content: design her research in such a way as to minimise difficulties.

items.analysis: Tony对Phoebe的研究经验反思表示赞赏，这体现在他使用"I like the way"和"well done"等积极词汇。他特别提到Phoebe能够"stepping back"（退一步思考）和"thinking about what you've learned"（思考所学到的东西），这正是对研究经验进行反思的表现。因此，选项B最准确地概括了Tony对Phoebe的印象。

groups.desc:
Questions 26-30
What did Phoebe find difficult about the different research techniques she used?
Choose FIVE answers from the box and write the correct letter A-G, next to questions 26-30.
Difficulties

list.title: F
list.content: Finding a suitable time to conduct the research

items.title: Observing lessons
items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是E（Processing data she had gathered），因为Tony在讨论课堂观察时提到了分析观察笔记是非常耗时的。"time consuming"（耗时）和"analysing"（分析）都表明了处理观察数据是困难的。这与选项E "Processing data she had gathered"（处理她收集到的数据）完全吻合，因为"Processing"（处理）在此上下文中可以视为"analysing"（分析）的同义词。

items.title: Interviewing teachers
items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是G（Getting hold of suitable equipment），因为Phoebe在描述采访过程时提到她需要等待高质量录音机可用。这表明她在获取合适的设备方面遇到了困难，符合选项G的描述。文中提到的"wait until one became available"直接对应了"Getting hold of"这个表述，而"high-quality recorder"则对应了"suitable equipment"。

items.title: Interviewing pupils
items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A（Obtaining permission），因为原文中提到一位老师对采访学生的伦理问题表示担忧，并且花了一段时间才同意让Phoebe采访学生。这表明获得采访许可是一个困难的过程，需要时间和说服。虽然最后老师同意了，但这个过程本身就反映了获得许可的困难。

items.title: Using questionnaires
items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是D（Working collaboratively），因为Phoebe在描述使用问卷调查时提到，她与另一个学生合作时遇到了困难。他们有不同的目标（agendas），导致工作时间延长了一倍。这明确表明了在合作过程中遇到的困难，符合题目要求的"Phoebe在使用不同研究技术时遇到的困难"。

items.title: Taking photographs
items.analysis: 文中Phoebe提到她在拍照时遇到的困难是希望每张照片都有明确的目的并能为研究做出贡献，但发现这很困难。这实际上就是在描述她在决定合适的拍摄重点（suitable focus）时遇到的困难。因此，选项B "Deciding on a suitable focus"最符合原文的描述。
